pages.title: Allied Health Sciences | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Bachelor of Science program in Health Science prepares students for a variety of roles within the allied health professions. The curriculum integrates coursework from disciplines across the University to provide students with a broad foundation of knowledge and skills related to life sciences, social sciences, communications, health ecology, public service, ethical practice, and data analysis.
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
About the Department of Allied Health Sciences
Professional Organizations Associated with Health Science
contentBlocks: The Bachelor of Science program in Health Science prepares students for a variety of roles within the allied health professions. The curriculum integrates coursework from disciplines across the University to provide students with a broad foundation of knowledge and skills related to life sciences, social sciences, communications, health ecology, public service, ethical practice, and data analysis.

This major also serves as home to all students preparing for the Bachelor of Science in Nursing here at Sac State. The curriculum is designed to provide the necessary pre-requisites to apply to the Nursing program or to pursue graduate school or advanced training in fields such as Nursing, Occupational Therapy, Sonography, Radiation Therapy, Physician Assistant School, Emergency Response, and more. The multi-disciplinary curriculum as well as a wide array of elective options offers a lot of flexibility for students to pursue an academic path that best matches their career goals.
For more information about the major, please visit our Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs).

pages.title: Spotlight | Sacramento State
pages.description: Celebrating achievements of Sac State's Allied Health Sciences alumni, students, faculty, and community supporters, driving impact and excellence.
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
The Happiness Walk: Finding Happiness in the, Sac State Urban Forest
contentBlocks: One of the most recognizable features of Sacramento State University is what can be described as its “urban forest.” As such, it offers anyone who takes a walk across campus a wonderful opportunity to get some exercise while enjoying the benefits that come from being attentive to the green world that provides the living context for the campus infrastructure.

Numerous studies have demonstrated that connecting with nature can yield a bevy of valued outcomes, both physical and mental, which combine to promote significant levels of happiness. This slide show describes a guided walk focused on our urban forest. We call it the Happiness Walk.

The Happiness Walk is designed to guide you on a a walk across campus, and in the process become familiar with some of the more impressive and unusual trees that make Sacramento State a remarkably beautiful campus.

You will be directed to “exemplar trees.” These are trees that have been chosen as examples of their species. Once you learn how to identify a species from your observations of an exemplar tree, you will be able to identify other trees of the same species. In this PowerPoint, a photo of each exemplar is provided and in some cases a close-up of the leaves.
Click the tree above to access the Happiness Walk PDF
The Happiness Walk: Finding Happiness in the, Sac State Urban Forest

links.text: The Happiness Walk: Finding Happiness in the, Sac State Urban Forest

pages.title: Frequently Asked Questions | Sacramento State
pages.description: Resources, advising information, and frequently asked questions for Expressed Interest (EI) Nursing Students new to Health Science.
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
Frequently Asked Questions about Health Science
Why should I major in Health Science?
What types of careers do Health Science majors pursue?
What should I do if I’m not sure what career I would like to pursue?
If I'm a Health Science Major does that mean I will get into Nursing?
What if I want to be a Nursing major, but am currently in Health Science
Where can I get additional questions answered about the Nursing Program?
Do I have to be a Health Science major to apply to the Nursing program?
What happens if I don’t get accepted into the Nursing program?
Where can I go if I need help with major advising or course scheduling?
How do I meet with a Health Science advisor?
How do I submit a course substitution?
How do I declare the Health Science major?
How do I declare a different major?
Does the HLSC program have a minimum grade requirement?
Does the BS in Health Science have a foreign language requirement?
How do I choose my BIO & CHEM lower-division courses?
Should I take a BIO and CHEM course during the same semester?
Can I take some of my science courses at a JC or Community College?
How do I know when I’m eligible to apply for graduation?
What are the graduation application deadlines?
contentBlocks: The Bachelor of Science program in Health Science is a multi-disciplinary undergraduate degree that provides students with a foundation to pursue careers in the Allied Health Professions. The curriculum also provides many (if not all) of the pre-requisites necessary to pursue advanced education to become a nurse, physician assistant, physical therapist, occupational therapist, cardiac rehabilitation specialist, sonographer, health educator, and more.

Earning a BS in Health Science may help students advance their current healthcare careers to the next level, develop professional skills sought by health care employers, and prepare for graduate or professional programs in the Allied Health Professions.
Below is a list of common careers that Health Science Majors pursue:
Registered health information technician

There are many resources on the Sac State Career Center webpage to help you find a career that is right for you. When you click on the Career Center webpage , you will find two important links. The first link is called “FOCUS 2 Career”, in which you will take a personality assessment that identifies careers that appear to match well with your unique characteristics. The second link is “What Can I Do With This Major”, which explores the professions relevant to a large variety of majors on and off campus.

No. If you are a Health Science Major, you will need to complete all pre-requisite requirements to submit a supplemental application to the nursing program. For more information, please visit the School of Nursing webpage .

At Sac State, all students who are interested in Nursing begin as Health Science majors. The Nursing program requires students to complete specific pre- and co-requisite courses as well as take the TEAS exam before they are eligible to apply for the Nursing program. More information about the Nursing program requirements and how to apply is available on the School of Nursing webpage . You may also visit the following links
Our School of Nursing has a live chat as well as group advising sessions. To access the live chat and gain additional advising information, please visit the School of Nursing webpage .

Being a Health Science major is not a pre-requisite to apply to the Nursing program at Sac State. However, the curriculum is designed to provide students with the pre-requisites necessary for the supplemental application.

The Nursing program only accepts up to 80 students per semester. If you don’t get accepted, do not be discouraged. You can finish your degree in Health Science (graduate with a BS in Health Science) and explore accelerated bachelor or master’s degree programs in Nursing. While Sac State does not offer a continuous Accelerated Second Bachelor of Science in Nursing or Entry Level Master’s track for post-baccalaureate students, we do offer a Second Bachelor’s degree, even when the University is closed to Second Bachelors (there is an exception for Nursing). If interested, please explore the School of Nursing webpage . For a list of accredited nursing programs, including Accelerated Second BSN or Entry Level Master’s programs offered in California, visit the California Board of Registered Nursing (BRN).

With a BS in Health Science, you may also be eligible to apply for other types of graduate or professional schools in the Allied Health Professions. Students are encouraged to seek “prospective student” advising with programs and professional schools of interest that are outside of Sacramento State University.

The Student Success Center in the College of Health and Human Services offers drop-in advising both online and in-person. The Student Success Center is located in Solano Hall, Room 3000. For hours of operation and links to virtual advising, please visit the HHS Student Success Center webpage .

Please join us for in-person or zoom drop-ins through the HHS Student Success Center. All our advisors are able to assist Health Science majors. Visit the Student Success Center website for our advising hours.

It is possible that you’ve taken a course at a community college or different university that you would like to use for your Health Science Major. It is also possible that you’d like to take a course that is not currently listed as a Health Science Elective. To determine whether you are eligible to make a course substitution, please visit with one of our advisors in the HHS Student Success Center. If the course is acceptable, our advisors will help you process the online forms.

If you want to declare Health Science, please attend a Health Science Change of Major workshop. All workshops are offered via zoom. Please select a date and complete the RSVP request. During the change of major workshop, we will discuss the common career paths for Health Science majors, major requirements, course planning, and the declaration process. It is recommended that this workshop be completed through a laptop or desktop computer.
If you are unable to attend a workshop, please email j.hernandez@csus.edu for information on declaring.

If you want to declare a major other than Health Science, please reach out to that specific department or the Student Success Center that services that major and ask about the procedures for declaring that major. There are 6 other Student Success Centers on the CSUS campus for majors that do not fall under the College of Health and Human Services.
Social Sciences & Interdisciplinary Studies

The Health Science program expects you get a C- or better in your required coursework. However, please note that many graduate and professional programs in the Allied Health Professions require a C or better in all pre-requisite courses.

Students graduating with a BS in Health Science will not be subject to the University’s Foreign Language Graduation Requirement. Students who change major may be subject to the University’s Foreign Language Graduation Requirement .
Freshman will be given a schedule that they can edit. For both freshman and transfer students, an advisor will work with you to select your best course options.
Biology and Chemistry courses are very challenging courses. It is highly recommended that students take one science course at a time to be as successful as possible in each course.

There are many courses that you can take at a JC that will transfer to Sac State. However, you should meet with an advisor before taking those courses to ensure that they will fulfill your major or pre-requisite requirements.
In order to apply for graduation, a student must meet the following criteria and file by the appropriate deadline:
You must have 85 units completed at the point of applying for graduation
You must have a declared Major on record
You must have an assigned Major advisor on record
You must be enrolled as an undergraduate student
Second bachelor's students must meet with a graduation advisor to obtain a graduation application
If you’re ready to apply, please complete the graduation application on the graduate advising webpage .
Please visit the graduation advising webpage to answer any graduation related questions.

pages.title: Welcome to Health Science | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Bachelor of Science program in Health Science prepares students for a variety of roles within the allied health professions. The curriculum integrates coursework from disciplines across the University to provide students with a broad foundation of knowledge and skills related to life sciences, social sciences, communications, health ecology, public service, ethical practice, and data analysis.
headings: Health Science College of Health & Human Services
contentBlocks: Welcome to Sacramento State! We are excited to have you in the Health Science program and look forward to helping you progress through the major, find your passion, and strive toward becoming the best you’re capable of becoming. The Health Science major is designed for students interested in pursuing careers in fields such as Nursing, Sonography, Radiation Therapy, Health Education, Occupational Therapy, Physician Assistant School, Emergency Response, and more.

Our curriculum offers a variety of courses to provide students with a foundation in the social and physical sciences, and we pride ourselves on individualizing students’ course of study to match their future career interests.

During your time at Sac State, it is my hope that you take advantage of our great campus resources such as the Career Center, the Well, and the Food Pantry. If you have any questions or need assistance, please feel free to contact us at healthscience@csus.edu or visit one of our advisors at the HHS Student Success Center on-campus or virtually.
We look forward to seeing what you accomplish and are proud that you choose to be #MadeAtSacState.

pages.title: Health Science Advising Resources | Sacramento State
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
contentBlocks: Health Science Advising Sheet (Fall 2021-Spring 2025 GE Catalog)
Health Science Advising Sheet (Fall 2025-Present GE Catalog)
Roadmap for Catalog Year Fall 2020 or Spring 2021
Roadmap for Catalog Year Fall 2021 or Later
Janette Hernandez, M.S. HHS Student Success Center Coordinator j.hernandez@csus.edu

Janette joined the Sac State team in Spring of 2022. Before coming to Sac State, she served undergraduate students as a dedicated academic advisor at California State University, Monterey Bay (CSUMB). Prior to her position at CSUMB, Janette held positions with two consecutive institutional grants from the U.S. Department of Education’s Developing Hispanic-Serving Institutions Program at California State University, Fullerton. She earned her Bachelors in Psychology and Social Behavior from UC Irvine, her masters in Clinical Psychology from CSU Fullerton, and is a licensed Marriage and Family Therapist. She is currently pursuing her doctorate in Educational Leadership at Sac State. She is proud to be part of the Sac State family and serving Sac State students through the College of Health and Human Services’ Student Success Center.
Joanna Sampanis, M.A. College Advisor & Retention Specialist sampanisj@csus.edu

Joanna Sampanis has joined the College of Health and Human Services as an advisor and specialist in the Student Success Center. She brings an extensive background in student services that includes academic advising, transfer credit articulation, and degree completion solutions. Joanna is “Made at Sac State” with degrees in Philosophy and English but her post-baccalaureate pursuits include educational technology, instructional design, and popular media. She enjoys working with students toward achieving their professional development goals through thoughtful academic planning and advocacy. Please feel free to check-in with her and share whatever interests you!
Alayna Short Administrative Support Coordinator, College of Health and Human Services ashort@csus.edu
Andrea Becker, Ph.D. Department of Allied Health Science Chair healthscience@csus.edu

Dr. Andrea Becker is the Chair of the Department of Allied Health Sciences and an Associate Professor (2017-present). She is an active member of the Association for Applied Sport Psychology and serves on the editorial board for the International Journal of Sports Science & Coaching. Outside of academia, Dr. Becker is a Sport Psychology Coach for the USA Men’s Volleyball Team (2013-present) and works with a number of other athletes at the professional and collegiate levels. Prior to Sac State, she spent ten years as a professor in the Department of Kinesiology at California State University, Fullerton (2007-2017), and also coached at UCLA (2012-2015) and UC, Irvine (2011-2012). A native of Martinez, California, Dr. Becker earned her PhD in Sport Studies from the University of Tennessee (2007), and her MS (2004) and BS (2001) degrees in Kinesiology and Sport Performance from Sacramento State. During her undergraduate studies, Becker played on the Sacramento State Softball team (1997-01). Go Hornets!

The new Health Science program (effective Fall 2020) is the new pathway towards Nursing. If students decide to complete the BS in Health Science it is strongly encouraged to select a minor to fulfill foundational background in an area of interest and to help meet all university unit requirements.
The information below provides units which may allow for the completion of a related minor in the four-year academic plan (two-year for transfers).
Please consult with the CHHS Student Success Center regarding the best academic plan for you.

pages.title: Health Science Internships | Sacramento State
pages.description: Provide internship information for students and potential students in the Department of Allied Health Sciences
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
Get a New Internship Site Approved
contentBlocks: Internships in the Department of Allied Health Sciences offer students an opportunity to earn academic credit while working with qualified professionals to gain hands-on experience in a variety of settings such as hospitals, healthcare systems, public agencies, community health and non-profit organizations, government offices, corporate wellness programs, universities and schools, recreation and fitness centers, and more.
Academic internships can be paid or unpaid depending on the site.
Students who participate in Allied Health Sciences internships are supervised by their faculty advisor as well as an on-site supervisor.
For every 1 unit of academic credit received, students must work a minimum of 45 hours.
Credit can only be obtained when interning at sites that are authorized by the University (see below for a list of pre-approved sites).
Student internship sites must be pre-approved by the University. On-campus sites such as the WELL or Sac State Athletics do not need additional authorization.

As of fall 2025 faculty, staff and students will now search for Community Partners in CEC Connect (CalState S4). For guidance on how to search for community partners, please visit Community Engagement Center (CEC) instructions page.
Contact the Department of Allied Health Sciences ( healthscience@csus.edu ) at least 5 months prior to the start of the internship to begin the site approval process.
In the email, provide the following site information:
Contact information (email and phone number) of site supervisor
Please note: Approval from the site or Procurement is not guaranteed.
Allied Health Sciences students must be individually hand-enrolled in internship courses. To get enrolled for credit, follow these steps:

Contact the internship site Introduce yourself as a Sac State student who is seeking an internship opportunity and ask if they are accepting interns for the semester in which you’d like to enroll. Ask if there are any requirements to be eligible for the internship (e.g., application process, interview, fingerprinting, etc.).
Introduce yourself as a Sac State student who is seeking an internship opportunity and ask if they are accepting interns for the semester in which you’d like to enroll.
Ask if there are any requirements to be eligible for the internship (e.g., application process, interview, fingerprinting, etc.).
If you are accepted by a business or organization to participate in their internship, complete the online Kinesiology Internship Learning Agreement.

Click on the form link and follow the instructions below. Step 1: Insert Student Emial address (CSUS email only) Step 2: Insert Internship Site Supervisor email address Step 3: Insert Faculty Advisor email address Step 4: Click Send and the Learning Agreement will appear
Step 1: Insert Student Emial address (CSUS email only)
Step 2: Insert Internship Site Supervisor email address
Step 3: Insert Faculty Advisor email address
Step 4: Click Send and the Learning Agreement will appear

Complete all required areas in the Allied Health Sciences Internship Agreement Form COURSE CODE : is based on your academic requirement or the type of internship you want to complete. The two most common examples for Allied Health students are HHS 195, RPTA 195D, or RPTA 195Q. Other course codes may be used if directed by your faculty advisor. UNIT LOAD: is based on how many internship hours you will complete during the semester. 1 unit = 45 hours of internship work 2 units = 90 hours of internship work 3 units = 135 hours of internship work 4 units = 180 hours of internship work 5 units = 225 hours of internship work 6 units =270 hours of internship work 7 units = 315 hours of internship work 8 units = 360 hours of internship work 9 units = 405 hours of internship work 10 units = 450 hours of internship work 11 units = 495 hours of internship work 12 units = 540 hours of internship work 13 units = 585 hours of internship work 14 units = 630 hours of internship work 15 units = 675 hours of internship work Once you complete all required areas and sign the Allied Health Sciences Internship Agreement Form, click submit.

COURSE CODE : is based on your academic requirement or the type of internship you want to complete. The two most common examples for Allied Health students are HHS 195, RPTA 195D, or RPTA 195Q. Other course codes may be used if directed by your faculty advisor.

UNIT LOAD: is based on how many internship hours you will complete during the semester. 1 unit = 45 hours of internship work 2 units = 90 hours of internship work 3 units = 135 hours of internship work 4 units = 180 hours of internship work 5 units = 225 hours of internship work 6 units =270 hours of internship work 7 units = 315 hours of internship work 8 units = 360 hours of internship work 9 units = 405 hours of internship work 10 units = 450 hours of internship work 11 units = 495 hours of internship work 12 units = 540 hours of internship work 13 units = 585 hours of internship work 14 units = 630 hours of internship work 15 units = 675 hours of internship work
Once you complete all required areas and sign the Allied Health Sciences Internship Agreement Form, click submit.

After submitting, the form will be routed to your site supervisor for signature, then to your faculty advisor for signature, and finally to the Department of Allied Health Sciences office. Upon receipt of a completed form, the Department of Allied Health Sciences office will enroll you in your internship for course credit. You do not have to do anything else at this time to get enrolled.
Please work with your faculty advisor to complete any assignments associated with your internship.

pages.title: Recreation Therapy | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information on the Recreation Therapy program
headings: Allied Health Sciences College of Health & Human Services
contentBlocks: The Recreation Therapy program at Sac State is the oldest in California, having begun in 1953. Starting in Fall 2020, the Recreation Therapy program became the first in the CSU to be administered as a stand-alone major, as opposed to a concentration, making your degree a B.S in Recreation Therapy.

The BS in Recreation Therapy focuses on recreation services for individuals who are ill, disabled, elderly, or incarcerated. It can be applied in clinical, transitional, and community based settings. Emphasis is on therapeutic recreation as it applies to children and adults who are physically, mentally, socially, and/or emotionally challenged.

Recreation Therapists must be certified by the National Council on Therapeutic Recreational Certification (NCTRC) in order to practice. The RT curriculum at Sacramento State prepares students to take the exam immediately after graduation. No additional preparation or requirements are necessary.
Please speak with an RT major advisor regarding certification.
Visit the Recreation Internships page for information regarding professional internships,
National Council on Therapeutic Recreation Certification
American Therapeutic Recreation Association
Certified Recreation Therapy Specialist (CTRS)
